MWCapture SDK  3.3.1.1556
  • Home

  • Release Notes
HDRCapture C++

It demonstrates how to use Magewell APIs to

  1. capture and render videos using Pro Capture Family Functions;
  2. preview HDR videos without an HDR display device using HDMIInfoFrame C++;
  3. record specified part of video to an MP4 file and have it hardware encoded by calling GPU Hardware Encoding.

Folder dir: SDKv3\Examples\VC++\GUI\HDRCapture

Supported Hardware

HDRCapture supports previewing high dynamic range (HDR) 10-bit signals with a non-HDR display device and record the signals as HDR MP4 files.

  • With modification of EDID:
    • Support video sources which support HDR playback and HDMI output, such as NVIDIA SHIELD, SONY PS4, etc.
    • Supported capture card:
      • Pro Capture HDMI 4K Plus
      • Pro Capture HDMI 4K Plus LT
      • Pro Capture Dual HDMI 4K Plus
      • Pro Capture AIO 4K Plus
    • NVIDIA's Pascal-architectured graphics cards, such as QUARDO P2000, with High Efficiency Video Coding (HEVC) Main10 hardware decoding support.

  • Without modification of EDID.

    • Support video sources which support HDR playback and HDMI output, such as NVIDIA SHIELD, SONY PS4, etc.
    • Supported capture card:
      • Pro Capture HDMI 4K Plus LT
      • Pro Capture Dual HDMI 4K Plus LT
    • HDR display devices, such as millet TV 4, SONY A8F, etc.
    • NVIDIA's Pascal-architectured graphics cards, such as QUARDO P2000, with HEVC Main10 hardware decoding support.

    Note: The above plan requires that the capture card have the Smart EDID function enabled and be connected to an HDR display device via the loop-through interface.

Workflow

Figure 1 shows how to use Magewell HDRCapture with modification of EDID.

Figure1 Using HDRCapture with modification of EDID
  1. Load EDID of an HDR display device to the capture card using AMCap2.
  2. Connect the HDMI output interface of the video source to the HDMI input interface of the capture card.
  3. The capture card outputs MWFOURCC_P010 (Planar, 4:2:0, 10-bit) to the graphics card for hardware encoding and display output.
  4. The graphics card encodes MWFOURCC_P010 to generate HEVC main10 streams, as well as performing HDR rendering, and outputting to a non-HDR display device.
  5. The MP4 recorder saves the HEVC main10 stream with HDR information as an MP4 file.

Figure 2 shows how to use MAGEWELL HDRCapture without modification of EDID.

Figure 2 Using HDRCapture without modification of EDID
  1. Connect the HDMI output of the video source to the HDMI input of Pro Capture HDMI 4K Plus LT.
  2. Connect the loop-through interface of the Pro Capture HDMI 4K Plus LT to the HDR display device.
  3. The capture card outputs MWFOURCC_P010 (Planar, 4:2:0, 10-bit) to the graphics card for hardware encoding and display output.
  4. The graphics card encodes MWFOURCC_P010 to generate HEVC main10 streams, as well as performing HDR rendering, and outputting to a non-HDR display device.
  5. The MP4 recorder saves the HEVC streams with HDR information as an MP4 file.

Procedure

Perform the following steps to preview and record an HDR signal without modifying EDID of the capture card.

  1. Mount a Pro Capture HDMI 4K+ in your computer, connect the HDMI input and output interfaces of the capture card with HDR devices.
  2. Download and install the latest driver of the NVIDIA graphics card and the latest Magewell SDK.
  3. Run HDRCapture.exe on OS x64. Click Device and choose 00-0 Pro Capture HDMI 4K+.
    Figure 3 Choose the capture card

  4. Click File, and select Preview HDR shown as figure 4, If you select Preview Auto, the preview effect will be the same as its original signal.

    Figure 4 Preview HDR

  5. Click File, and select Preview SDR. The render effect is shown as figure 5.

    Figure 5 Preview SDR

  6. Click File. Select Preview Auto, the preview effect will be the same as its original signal. Select Preview HDR if the input is HDR signal, select Preview SDR if the input is SDR.
  7. Set Luminance and Threshold for HDR.
    Figure 6 HDR settings
  8. Click File. Select Start HDR Recording, specify the file name and path.
    Figure 7 HDR Start Record
  9. Click File. Select Stop HDR Recording to stop recording.
    Figure 8 HDR Stop Record

Verification

Do any of the following to playback the recorded MP4 files:

  • Use the mpv (media player software, official website: https://mpv.io/).
  • Use an HDR hardware playback device, such as a NVIDIA SHIELD, and an HDR display device, such as a Mi LED Smart TV 4.
